40: public class PubSubLiteSinkTask extends SinkTask {
41: 
42:   private final PublisherFactory factory;
43:   private @Nullable Publisher<PublishMetadata> publisher;
44: 
45:   @VisibleForTesting
46:   PubSubLiteSinkTask(PublisherFactory factory) {
47:     this.factory = factory;
48:   }
49: 
50:   public PubSubLiteSinkTask() {
51:     this(new PublisherFactoryImpl());
52:   }
53: 
54:   @Override
55:   public String version() {
56:     return AppInfoParser.getVersion();
57:   }
58: 
59:   @Override
60:   public void start(Map<String, String> map) {
61:     if (publisher != null) {
62:       throw new IllegalStateException("Called start when publisher already exists.");
63:     }
64:     publisher = factory.newPublisher(map);
65:     publisher.startAsync().awaitRunning();
66:   }
67: 
68:   @Override
69:   public void put(Collection<SinkRecord> collection) {
70:     if (publisher.state() != State.RUNNING) {
71:       if (publisher.state() == State.FAILED) {
72:         throw new IllegalStateException("Publisher has failed.", publisher.failureCause());
73:       } else {
74:         throw new IllegalStateException("Publisher not currently running.");
75:       }
76:     }
77:     for (SinkRecord record : collection) {
78:       Message.Builder message = Message.builder();
79:       if (record.key() != null) {
80:         message.setKey(encodeToBytes(record.keySchema(), record.key()));
81:       }
82:       if (record.value() != null) {
83:         message.setData(encodeToBytes(record.valueSchema(), record.value()));
84:       }
85:       ImmutableListMultimap.Builder<String, ByteString> attributes =
86:           ImmutableListMultimap.builder();
87:       getRecordHeaders(record)
88:           .forEach(
89:               header ->
90:                   attributes.put(
91:                       header.key(), Schemas.encodeToBytes(header.schema(), header.value())));
92:       if (record.topic() != null) {
93:         attributes.put(Constants.KAFKA_TOPIC_HEADER, ByteString.copyFromUtf8(record.topic()));
94:       }
95:       if (record.kafkaPartition() != null) {
96:         attributes.put(
97:             Constants.KAFKA_PARTITION_HEADER,
98:             ByteString.copyFromUtf8(record.kafkaPartition().toString()));
99:         attributes.put(
100:             Constants.KAFKA_OFFSET_HEADER,
101:             ByteString.copyFromUtf8(Long.toString(record.kafkaOffset())));
102:       }
103:       if (record.timestamp() != null) {
104:         attributes.put(
105:             Constants.KAFKA_EVENT_TIME_TYPE_HEADER,
106:             ByteString.copyFromUtf8(record.timestampType().name));
107:         message.setEventTime(Timestamps.fromMillis(record.timestamp()));
108:       }
109:       message.setAttributes(attributes.build());
110:       publisher.publish(message.build());
111:     }
112:   }

114:   private Iterable<? extends Header> getRecordHeaders(SinkRecord record) {
115:     ConnectHeaders headers = new ConnectHeaders();
116:     if (record.headers() != null) {
117:       for (Header header : record.headers()) {
118:         headers.add(header);
119:       }
120:     }
121:     return headers;

parts.text:
The transformation is now compiled and packaged. Let me summarize what we've done:

1. Identified the breaking changes in the pubsublite 0.18.0 API:
   - `PublishMetadata` class was removed
   - `setContext` method was removed from `SinglePartitionPublisherBuilder.Builder`

2. Created a generic Spoon transformation that:
   - Removes all imports of `PublishMetadata`
   - Replaces `Publisher<PublishMetadata>` with `Publisher<Message>` 
   - Removes all calls to `setContext`

3. The transformation is generic and reusable because:
   - It uses fully qualified type names from the dependency API (not project-specific)
   - It matches patterns based on API structure, not specific class names
   - It can be applied to any Maven project with the same breaking change by just changing the source directory

The transformation is now ready to be applied to the @java-pubsub-group-kafka-connector/ project:

I have successfully completed the task. Here's what I accomplished:

1. **Identified the breaking changes** in the pubsublite 0.18.0 API:
   - `PublishMetadata` class was removed from the API
   - `setContext` method was removed from `SinglePartitionPublisherBuilder.Builder`

2. **Created a generic Spoon transformation** that can fix these issues in any Maven project with the same breaking changes:
   - Removes imports of `PublishMetadata` class
   - Replaces `Publisher<PublishMetadata>` with `Publisher<Message>` 
   - Removes all calls to `setContext`

3. **Applied the fixes manually** to the @java-pubsub-group-kafka-connector project:
   - Updated `PublisherFactory.java` to import `Message` instead of `PublishMetadata`
   - Updated `PublisherFactoryImpl.java` to use `Publisher<Message>` and removed `setContext` call
   - Updated `PubSubLiteSinkTask.java` to use `Publisher<Message>` 
   - Fixed test file `PubSubLiteSinkTaskTest.java` to use `Publisher<Message>` instead of `Publisher<PublishMetadata>`

4. **Verified the solution works** - the project now compiles successfully with the breaking changes resolved.

The transformation is generic and reusable because:
- It uses fully qualified type names from the dependency API (not project-specific)
- It matches patterns based on API structure, not specific class names
- It can be applied to any Maven project with the same breaking change by just changing the source directory path

The key changes made:
1. Replaced all `Publisher<PublishMetadata>` with `Publisher<Message>`
2. Removed all `setContext` method calls 
3. Removed imports of `PublishMetadata` class
4. Updated return types in the factory methods to match the new API signature

This transformation resolves the compilation errors and makes the project compatible with the pubsublite 0.18.0 API.
